rationale: The cited paper appears directly relevant to the citing paper’s core mechanism: both focus on attention/information-processing changes during conjoint-style elicitation and links to external validity, using eye-tracking evidence (citing Study 3 and cited Study 3 both involve laptop conjoint tasks with external-validity choices). The citing note explicitly positions its contribution as complementing prior process-tracing work on adaptation, which the cited paper provides (carryover effects and changed partworth estimates), so this is plausibly substantive rather than a generic mention.
